description: The current concession rules for non-atomic, non-energy minerals, aligned with the auction regime, covering grant, operation, transfer and termination of mineral concessions.

title: Atomic Minerals Concession Rules, 2016
description: Govern reconnaissance, prospecting and mining concessions for atomic minerals (uranium, thorium, beryllium, etc.), administered with the Department of Atomic Energy.

title: Mineral Conservation and Development Rules, 2017
description: Prescribe scientific and sustainable mining practices, mining plans, and environmental safeguards. Supersede the 1988 rules.

title: Mineral (Auction) Rules, 2015
description: Lay down the electronic-auction procedure for grant of mining leases and composite licences, including bid parameters and payment of the auction premium. Amended in 2024 to operationalise exploration licences.

title: Mineral (Auction) Amendment Rules, 2024
description: Amend the 2015 auction rules to set the framework for granting exploration licences by descending reverse auction and for paying the exploration licensee a share of the future auction premium.

title: Minerals (Evidence of Mineral Contents) Rules, 2015
description: Prescribe the exploration levels (G4-G1) and evidence of mineral contents required before a block can be auctioned, standardising pre-auction geoscientific data.

title: Minerals (Transfer of Mining Lease Granted Otherwise than through Auction for Captive Purpose) Rules, 2016
description: Set out the mechanism and transfer charges for transferring non-auctioned captive mining leases, enabling M&A in mineral-using industries.

title: Minerals (Other than Atomic and Hydro Carbons Energy Minerals) Concession Amendment Rules, 2024
description: Notified vide G.S.R. 50(E) to operationalise the exploration-licence regime, clarify disposal of minerals below threshold value, and rationalise penalties for small miners.

title: National Mineral Exploration Trust (NMET) Rules, 2015
description: Establish and govern the NMET, funded by statutory contributions from mining lessees, to finance regional and detailed mineral exploration, including of critical minerals.

title: National Mineral Policy, 2019
description: The overarching policy for the mining sector, aiming to increase exploration and production, ensure sustainable and responsible mining, and reduce import dependence for critical and strategic minerals.

title: The Drone Rules, 2021
description: Liberalised rules for unmanned aircraft systems - a Digital Sky platform, airspace zones (green/yellow/red), simplified registration and type certification, and eased operating requirements. They replaced the UAS Rules, 2021.
compliance: UAS registration (UIN); Remote Pilot Certificate; type cert; Digital Sky; zones

title: The Aircraft Act, 1934
description: The long-standing aviation statute under which drones were first regulated (via the Drone Rules). Repealed and replaced by the Bharatiya Vayuyan Adhiniyam, 2024.

title: The Bharatiya Vayuyan Adhiniyam, 2024
description: The successor aviation statute replacing the Aircraft Act, 1934. It is the current parent legislation for civil aviation and unmanned aircraft systems, from which drone rule-making and enforcement now flow.

title: Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) Rules, 2021
description: The first dedicated drone rules, notified in March 2021 with heavy restrictions and prohibited areas. Superseded within months by the liberalised Drone Rules, 2021.
compliance: UAS registration & operating restrictions; prohibited areas

title: Drone (Amendment) Rules, 2022
description: Replaced the Remote Pilot Licence with a Remote Pilot Certificate, extended registration timelines, and eased other requirements to promote adoption.
compliance: UAS registration; Remote Pilot Certificate; Digital Sky compliance

title: Drone (Amendment) Rules, 2023
description: Expanded the list of acceptable identity documents for drone registration and made further procedural relaxations.
compliance: UAS registration; Remote Pilot Certificate; Digital Sky compliance

title: India's Airspace Map for Drone Operations, 2021
description: Published the interactive airspace map on the Digital Sky platform, dividing airspace into green, yellow and red zones to govern where drones may fly and with what clearances.
compliance: Check green/yellow/red zone & clearances before each flight

title: PLI Scheme for Drones and Drone Components, 2021
description: A production-linked incentive scheme by the Ministry of Civil Aviation to build domestic drone and drone-component manufacturing capacity.

title: Drone Import Policy, 2022
description: A DGFT notification prohibiting the import of foreign drones (in CBU/SKD/CKD form) except for R&D, defence and security purposes, to protect domestic manufacturing and address security concerns.
compliance: Import ban on foreign drones except R&D/defence/security

title: Draft Civil Drone (Promotion and Regulation) Bill, 2025
description: A draft standalone drone statute to replace the Drone Rules, mandating registration, type certification and airspace-zone compliance, with stronger penalties and a compensation regime. Applies to foreign persons operating in India.
compliance: (draft) UIN registration; type certification; zone compliance

title: The Bureau of Indian Standards Act, 2016
description: Makes the Bureau of Indian Standards the national standards body, with powers to set standards, run conformity-assessment and certification schemes, and enforce compulsory certification for notified goods, including many electronics and IT products.
maxPenalty: Up to 2 yrs &/or fine >= Rs 2 lakh (first offence)

title: The Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) Act, 2023
description: Establishes the Anusandhan National Research Foundation as India's apex body to fund, coordinate and promote research and development across science, technology and innovation, drawing government and private funding and subsuming the earlier SERB.

title: NavIC / Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System (IRNSS)
description: India's indigenous regional satellite-navigation system (7-satellite constellation) providing Standard Positioning Service (civilian) and an encrypted Restricted Service (strategic/military) over India and ~1,500 km around it - reducing dependence on foreign GNSS such as GPS.

title: GAGAN (GPS Aided GEO Augmented Navigation)
description: A satellite-based augmentation system jointly built by ISRO and the Airports Authority of India to improve GPS accuracy and integrity for safety-of-life civil-aviation use, interoperable with US WAAS, European EGNOS and Japanese MSAS.

title: NavIC device-support mandate (phased)
description: An administrative push by the government (DoT/MeitY, with industry) requiring smartphones and certain devices sold in India to support NavIC signals (initially L5, then L1) to build a domestic PNT user base. Implemented through phased engagement with manufacturers; timelines have shifted.

title: Cable Landing Station (CLS) licensing framework (ILD / Unified License)
description: The regime under which only International Long Distance / ISP-A licensees may set up cable landing stations and land submarine cables in Indian territorial waters, with lawful-interception and security conditions - the operative framework for subsea connectivity into India.

title: TRAI Recommendations on Licensing Framework and Regulatory Mechanism for Submarine Cable Landing in India, 2023
description: TRAI recommendations proposing Main CLS and CLS point-of-presence categories, open/non-discriminatory access, Indian-flagged repair vessels, and recognition of submarine cables and CLS as critical infrastructure of national importance - a live subsea-connectivity and resilience agenda.

title: National Digital Communications Policy (NDCP), 2018
description: The umbrella telecom/digital-communications policy (successor to NTP-2012) targeting universal broadband, investment, spectrum reform, and a customer-focused, secure digital-communications ecosystem - the strategic backdrop to the Telecommunications Act, 2023.

title: National Frequency Allocation Plan (NFAP), 2025
description: The WPC/DoT master plan for radio-spectrum allocation across services, updated to reflect WRC-2023 and ITU Radio Regulations 2024 - identifying bands for 5G/6G (incl. 6425-7125 MHz), satellite (Ka/Q/V), V2X and IoT. Successor to NFAP-2018 and NFAP-2022.

title: Bharat 6G Vision and Mission, 2023
description: The national vision to make India a leader in designing, developing and deploying 6G by 2030, built on ubiquitous coverage, affordability and sustainability, and driving the Bharat 6G Alliance and indigenous 6G R&D.

title: M2M / IoT Service Provider Registration framework, 2022
description: A DoT framework requiring registration of Machine-to-Machine Service Providers and WPAN/WLAN connectivity providers, bringing IoT/M2M communication services into the telecom regulatory perimeter.

title: National Electric Mobility Mission Plan (NEMMP) 2020
description: The foundational plan (2013) setting India's electric and hybrid mobility roadmap and targets, under which the FAME schemes were launched.

title: FAME India Scheme, Phase I, 2015
description: Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of (Hybrid &) Electric Vehicles, Phase I - demand incentives and pilot charging infrastructure. Concluded and succeeded by Phase II.

title: FAME India Scheme, Phase II, 2019
description: FAME Phase II (Rs 11,500 crore) - purchase incentives for EVs (especially 2W/3W and e-buses) and public charging infrastructure. Concluded March 2024 and succeeded by PM E-DRIVE.

title: PLI Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Storage, 2021
description: Production-linked incentive to build ~50 GWh of domestic advanced-battery-cell manufacturing, reducing import dependence for lithium-ion and next-generation cells.

title: PLI Scheme for Automobile and Auto Components (PLI-Auto), 2021
description: Incentivises domestic manufacture of advanced automotive technology products, including electric and hydrogen-fuel-cell vehicles and their components.

title: PM E-DRIVE Scheme, 2024
description: PM Electric Drive Revolution in Innovative Vehicle Enhancement - the successor to FAME II, focused on e-2W/3W, e-buses, e-trucks, e-ambulances and public charging infrastructure.

title: Guidelines for Installation and Operation of EV Charging Infrastructure, 2024
description: Ministry of Power guidelines setting standards and protocols for a connected, interoperable EV charging (and battery-swapping) network, including tariffs and technical requirements.
compliance: Interoperable charging standards; safety; tariff norms

title: Draft Battery Swapping Policy, 2022
description: A NITI Aayog draft to standardise and promote battery-as-a-service and swapping for 2W/3W, including interoperability and safety standards. Consultation held; not finalised as a standalone policy.

title: Battery Waste Management Rules, 2022
description: MoEFCC rules under the Environment (Protection) Act placing Extended Producer Responsibility on battery producers for collection, recycling and reuse (including EV lithium-ion batteries) - central to a critical-minerals circular economy.
compliance: EPR: collect, recycle and report battery waste

title: National Green Hydrogen Mission, 2023
description: MNRE mission to make India a global hub for green-hydrogen production, use and export - via the SIGHT incentive programme, pilots, R&D (SHIP) and a standards/regulation framework; target ~5 MMT/annum by 2030 with ~125 GW added renewables.

title: Jawaharlal Nehru National Solar Mission (National Solar Mission), 2010
description: The flagship mission to scale grid-connected and off-grid solar power and domestic solar manufacturing, repeatedly scaled up as part of India's renewable-energy and clean-tech push.

title: PLI Scheme for High-Efficiency Solar PV Modules, 2021
description: Production-linked incentive to build integrated, high-efficiency solar photovoltaic module manufacturing in India (polysilicon-to-module), reducing import dependence on solar cells and modules.

title: Electricity (Promoting Renewable Energy through Green Energy Open Access) Rules, 2022
description: Ministry of Power rules under the Electricity Act enabling open access to green (renewable) electricity for large consumers, supporting clean-energy procurement including for green hydrogen and data centres.
compliance: Apply for green open access; RE procurement

title: Make in India / Make in India 2.0
description: The flagship initiative to make India a global manufacturing hub, expanded (2.0) to 27 sectors with a focus on advanced technologies; the policy umbrella under which the sectoral PLI schemes sit.

title: National Manufacturing Mission, 2025
description: A mission-mode programme providing a unified vision for manufacturing competitiveness, explicitly targeting emerging areas - EV batteries, green hydrogen, solar PV, advanced materials - and Industry 4.0 technologies (AI, robotics, digital twins).

title: National Strategy for Additive Manufacturing, 2022
description: A MeitY strategy to develop India's additive-manufacturing (3D-printing) ecosystem - domestic machines, materials and software, standards, and adoption across strategic sectors.

title: SAMARTH Udyog Bharat 4.0
description: A Department of Heavy Industry initiative establishing Industry-4.0 demonstration and capability centres to spread smart-manufacturing technologies (IoT, robotics, analytics) among Indian industry, especially MSMEs.

title: Startup India Initiative, 2016
description: The national programme to build India's startup ecosystem - recognition, tax and compliance benefits, Fund of Funds, and self-certification - foundational to deep-tech and technology entrepreneurship.

title: National Deep Tech Startup Policy (NDTSP), 2023 (draft)
description: A draft policy (Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser) to nurture deep-tech startups - in AI, quantum, semiconductors, space, biotech - through funding, IP support, procurement and R&D infrastructure. Under consultation.

title: Draft Digital Competition Bill, 2024
description: An ex-ante competition framework (Ministry of Corporate Affairs) modelled on the EU Digital Markets Act. It would let the CCI designate Systemically Significant Digital Enterprises and bar self-preferencing, anti-steering, data misuse, tying and third-party-app restrictions, with penalties up to 10% of global turnover.

title: Draft National Data Centre Policy, 2020
description: A MeitY draft to make India a global data-centre hub - infrastructure status, single-window clearances, data-centre economic zones and incentives. Consultation held; not finalised.

title: Digital Agriculture Mission, 2024
description: A Ministry of Agriculture mission to build digital public infrastructure for agriculture - the AgriStack, Krishi Decision Support System and soil-profile maps - to enable data-driven, tech-enabled farming and services.
compliance: Consent-based farmer data; interoperable agri DPI

title: AgriStack (farmer registry and agri data infrastructure)
description: The core agri digital-public-infrastructure: a consent-based farmers' registry, geo-referenced village maps and crop-sown registry, intended to underpin agritech services, credit and subsidies.

title: Medical Devices Rules, 2017
description: Rules under the Drugs and Cosmetics Act (administered by CDSCO) that regulate medical devices - including software as a medical device - through risk-based classification, licensing, clinical investigation and quality requirements.
compliance: Risk-based device licensing; quality system; clinical investigation approval

title: New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules, 2019
description: Rules governing approval of new drugs and the conduct of clinical trials (including for biologics and biotech products), with ethics-committee oversight and timelines - central to pharma-tech and biotech product development.

title: Telemedicine Practice Guidelines, 2020
description: Guidelines (issued with the Board of Governors/MCI and MoHFW) enabling registered medical practitioners to provide consultations remotely, giving legal standing to telemedicine in India.

title: National Digital Health Blueprint / Mission (ABDM foundation), 2019
description: The blueprint that laid the architecture for India's digital-health ecosystem - health IDs, registries and interoperable records - operationalised through the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
